description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel stereo sound system housed in a rectangular black case with a hinged lid lined in red fabric. The front features two large metallic reels, a set of analog dials, and several control knobs, all arranged symmetrically. The case appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with visible wear and metallic hardware, including latches and a carrying handle on top. The sound system sits atop a simple, boxy wooden pedestal or crate, which serves as its base. The interior of the lid is lined with a plush red material, providing a striking contrast to the otherwise utilitarian exterior. The overall design suggests a robust, portable audio device from the mid-20th century, likely used for recording or playback in urban environments.
description: A vintage reel-to-reel stereo sound system in a black case with a red-lined lid, featuring large reels, analog dials, and control knobs, set atop a wooden pedestal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open red-lined lid and the depth of the black case, with the wooden pedestal visible below.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the solid black back of the case and the wooden pedestal, with the handle and latches on top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the angled open lid and the thickness of the case, with the pedestal beneath.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the two large reels, analog dials, control knobs, and the open red-lined lid, with the wooden pedestal as a base.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is located on the front, featuring two large reels, several round metallic knobs, analog dials, and a few buttons, all symmetrically arranged.
